content.problemStatement:
Given an undirected graph with NNN vertices and MMM edges, determine if there exists a Hamiltonian path (a path that visits each vertex exactly once) in the graph.


Input Format

First line contains TTT, the number of test cases
For each test case:

First line contains NNN and MMM
Next MMM lines contain pairs of integers representing edges




Output Format

For each test case, print 1 if Hamiltonian path exists, 0 otherwise.
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains two integers N and M — the number of vertices (1‑indexed) and the number of undirected edges. The next M lines each contain two integers u and v (1 ≤ u, v ≤ N) describing an edge between vertices u and v.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing 1 if the graph contains a Hamiltonian path (a path that visits every vertex exactly once) and 0 otherwise.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: The graph 1‑2‑3‑4 with an extra edge 2‑4 contains the Hamiltonian path 1→2→3→4. Test case 2: Vertices {1,2,3,4} with edges (1,2), (2,3), (2,4) cannot be visited exactly once in a single path, so the answer is 0.
A single vertex trivially forms a Hamiltonian path of length 1.
The complete graph on three vertices has many Hamiltonian paths, e.g., 1→2→3.

solution.approach: Because N ≤ 10, we can afford exponential search. For each possible start vertex we run a depth‑first search that marks visited vertices. At each step we move to an unvisited neighbour. If we have visited N vertices we have found a Hamiltonian path. The search is backtracked correctly, so the total work per test case is O(N·2^N) which is trivial for N≤10. This simple backtracking is fast enough and easy to implement.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N·2^N) per test case (worst‑case N=10 ⇒ ≤10·1024 operations)
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for the visited array and recursion stack

code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{static int N;static boolean[][] adj;static boolean dfs(int v,boolean[] visited,int cnt){visited[v]=true;if(cnt==N)return true;for(int nxt=1;nxt<=N;nxt++){if(adj[v][nxt]&&!visited[nxt]){if(dfs(nxt,visited,cnt+1))return true;visited[nxt]=false;}}return false;}static boolean hasHamiltonianPath(){boolean[] visited=new boolean[N+1];for(int start=1;start<=N;start++){Arrays.fill(visited,false);if(dfs(start,visited,1))return true;}return false;}public static void main(String[] args)throws Exception{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder();int T=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());while(T-- >0){StringTokenizer st=new StringTokenizer(br.readLine());N=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());int M=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());adj=new boolean[N+1][N+1];for(int i=0;i<M;i++){st=new StringTokenizer(br.readLine());int u=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());int v=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());adj[u][v]=adj[v][u]=true;}sb.append(hasHamiltonianPath()?1:0).append('\n');}System.out.print(sb.toString());}}
